title: Fiery Smoked Argentinian Beef Skewers
description: Spicy Argentinian dish featuring ground almonds and smoked beef, perfect for adventurous eaters, this low-carb friendly recipe combines the flavors of South America with a cheesy twist, the cheese-pull effect is achieved with melted mozzarella.
instructions: Preheat the grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine ground beef, ground almonds, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Thread the beef mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and grill for 10 minutes, turning occasionally.
After 10 minutes, brush the skewers with chipotle peppers in adobo sauce and continue grilling for an additional 5 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the skewers from the grill and top each with shredded mozzarella cheese.
Return the skewers to the grill and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
